{"success": true, "name": "nexa-paraguay-deployment", "file": "references/section-registration-pitfall.md", "content": "# Empty Page Due to Unregistered Section (Contacto Fix)

## Symptom
A page renders only header + footer. The `<main>` section is empty or has only a generic "Book free consultation" link. No content blocks appear between header and footer.

## Root Cause
The page config in `nexa-pages/{slug}.json` references `sections[].id` values that don't exist in the `SECTIONS` map inside `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx`. The fallback is `GenericSection`, which only renders if the data has array fields (`items`, `groups`, `pillars`, `members`, `paragraphs`, `trust.items`). Data with flat fields like `title`, `subtitle`, `email`, `whatsapp` produces no output.

## Diagnosis

### Step 1: Check page config
```bash
cat nexa-pages/contacto.json
# Look at sections[].id values
```

### Step 2: Check SECTIONS map
```bash
grep "const SECTIONS" -A 30 src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx | head -40
```

### Step 3: Check if component exists but isn't registered
```bash
grep "ComponentName" src/components/*.tsx  # find the actual export
```

## Fix: Add missing sections to SECTIONS map

In `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx`:

```typescript
import { BookingEmbedSection, ContactDetailsSection } from './sections-extra'
import { BookingFormSection } from './BookingFormSection'

const SECTIONS: Record<string, any> = {
  // ... existing sections ...
  'booking-embed': BookingEmbedSection,
  contact: ContactDetailsSection,
  'contact-form': ContactDetailsSection,
  'contact-details': ContactDetailsSection,
  'booking-form': BookingFormSection,
}
```

## Locale-Aware Components

When a section component uses hardcoded Spanish fallbacks (e.g., `'Agenda tu consulta gratuita'`), it won't localize. Fix pattern:

1. Accept `locale` prop (passed automatically by SectionsRenderer)
2. Read content from `data` prop (which is locale-resolved via `resolveContent`)
3. For multi-locale data shapes, resolve at render time:
   ```typescript
   const lang = locale || 'es'
   const title = typeof d.title === 'object' ? (d.title[lang] || d.title.es) : d.title
   ```
4. Add locale-specific field labels to the content JSON under `contactPage.booking`:
   ```json
   {
     "stepLabels": ["Program", "Your Details", "Confirm"],
     "step1Title": "Choose your program",
     "nameLabel": "Full name",
     ...
   }
   ```

## Content Data Shapes

The booking content in `content/{locale}.json` under `contactPage.booking`:

- `title`, `subtitle` — section headings
- `ctaLabel` — button text (BookingEmbedSection reads `ctaText || ctaLabel`)
- `bookingUrl` — CTA href (BookingEmbedSection reads `bookingUrl || ctaHref`)
- `programs[]` — array of program objects with 4-locale `{es, en, nl, de}` objects for `title`, `subtitle`, `duration`
- `stepLabels`, `step1Title`, `nameLabel`, etc. — form field labels per locale
- `fallbackWhatsapp`, `fallbackEmail` — fallback contact for unavailable calendar

## Deploy Verification

```bash
# Build locally
npm run build
docker build --no-cache -t nexa-paraguay:prod .
docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web

# Verify each locale
curl -sL https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/es/contacto | grep -c "Agende su consulta"
curl -sL https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/en/contacto | grep -c "Book your consultation"
curl -sL https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/nl/contacto | grep -c "Plan uw consult"
curl -sL https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/de/contacto | grep -c "Gespräch buchen"
```

Components in `FeedbackSection` use `data` directly (e.g., `data.title`). | Component | What it reads | Correct content key | |-----------|--------------|-------------------| | HeroSection | `pageContent.hero` | `\"home\"` | | TrustSection | `pageContent.trust` | `\"home\"` | | ServicesSection | `d.services?.groups \|\| d.groups` | `\"home\"` | | WhyCountrySection | `pageContent.whyCountry` | `\"home\"` | | ProcessSection | `pageContent.process` | `\"home\"` | | FeedbackSection | `data.title`, `data.eyebrow` | `\"home.feedback\"` | | CtaBanner | `pageContent.finalCta \|\| pageContent.cta` | `\"home\"` | If a section returns null, check the content key depth against what the component reads.", "new_string": "## App Router Architecture (current) - Framework: Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TypeScript 5, Tailwind v4 - Routes use `[locale]/[slug]` pattern — 4 locales (es/en/nl/de), 25+ slug pages - Content JSON-driven: `content/{locale}.json` (4 locales, `es.json` is source of truth at ~208KB), `nexa-pages/*.json` (26+ page configs), `images.json` (image registry) - SectionsRenderer: `'use client'` component with 30+ sections loaded via `dynamic(() => import(...), { ssr: true })` - PPR disabled (`cacheComponents` removed) — all routes are `● SSG` static HTML - Each section component receives `pageContent`, `data`, `images`, and `locale` props - Section content keys in page configs reference dot-paths into the locale JSON (e.g. `\"home.hero\"`) - Dynamic routes using `generateStaticParams()` — all pages pre-rendered at build time - Docker Swarm deployment with standalone output - **EN/NL/DE JSON is polluted** — flat empty-string keys (`aboutPage: ""`) accumulate at every nesting level in contactPage, hours, footer. Components must use explicit property access, not `Object.keys()` or `Object.values()`. ### SECTIONS Map (SectionsRenderer.tsx) All renderable sections are registered in `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx`. Source files: - `src/components/sections.tsx` — HeroSection, TrustSection, WhyCountrySection, ServicesSection, ProcessSection, CtaBanner, RequirementsSection + StatsSection, TestimonialsSection, ProgramsSection, FeaturesSection - `src/components/sections-extra.tsx` — BookingEmbedSection, ContactDetailsSection, CaseStudySection, FaqSection, BlogSection, TeamSection, StorySection, PillarsSection, PageHeroSection, GallerySection, GlossarySection, GuidesSection, HighlightSection, IntakeWizardSection, NewsletterSection, PressReleasesListSection, ServiceDetailSection, ComparisonSection, PrivacyAccordion, FaqSearchSection - `src/components/BookingFormSection.tsx` — 3-step intake wizard (locale-aware, reads programs + labels from content) ```typescript const SECTIONS: Record = { hero: HeroSection, 'trust-signals': TrustSection, 'why-destination': WhyCountrySection, services: ServicesSection, 'process-timeline': ProcessSection, 'cta-banner': CtaBanner, requirements: RequirementsSection, 'booking-embed': BookingEmbedSection, contact: ContactDetailsSection, 'contact-form': ContactDetailsSection, 'contact-details': ContactDetailsSection, 'booking-form': BookingFormSection, } ``` **PITFALL: Unregistered section IDs → empty page.** If a page config's `sections[].id` isn't in SECTIONS, it falls through to GenericSection which only renders for data with array fields (items, groups, pillars, etc). Flat data (title, subtitle, email) renders **nothing**. No error, no visible feedback. See `references/section-registration-pitfall.md`. **Adding a new section:** import the component, add to SECTIONS. The `id` is the string used in nexa-pages JSON. ### Locale-Aware Component Pattern When creating a section that needs locale-specific text: 1. Import `locale` from props — SectionsRenderer passes it automatically 2. For multi-locale content data (stored as `{"es": "...", "en": "...", ...}` objects), resolve at render time 3. For field labels (step titles, button text), store in content JSON under the section's content path 4. Provide English-default fallbacks for each label ```typescript export function BookingFormSection({ data, locale }: any) { const lang = locale || 'es' // Resolve 4-locale program data const title = typeof item.title === 'object' ? item.title[lang] || item.title.es : item.title // Use content-provided labels with fallbacks const t = (key: string, fallback: string) => d[key] || fallback return

{t('step1Title', 'Choose your program')}

{"success": true, "name": "nexa-paraguay-deployment", "description": "Build and deploy the Nexa Paraguay site to Docker Swarm on VPS 72.61.44.159", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "--- name: nexa-paraguay-deployment description: Build and deploy the Nexa Paraguay site to Docker Swarm on VPS 72.61.44.159 --- # Nexa Paraguay Deployment ## App Router Architecture (current) - Framework: Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TypeScript 5, Tailwind v4 - Routes use `[locale]/[slug]` pattern — 4 locales (es/en/nl/de), 25+ slug pages - Content JSON-driven: `content/{locale}.json` (4 locales, `es.json` is source of truth at ~208KB), `nexa-pages/*.json` (26+ page configs), `images.json` (image registry) - SectionsRenderer: `'use client'` component with 30+ sections loaded via `dynamic(() => import(...), { ssr: true })` - PPR disabled (`cacheComponents` removed) — all routes are `● SSG` static HTML - Each section component receives `pageContent` (the resolved content sub-object) and `data` (same, as fallback) - Section content keys in page configs reference dot-paths into the locale JSON (e.g. `"home.hero"`) - Dynamic routes using `generateStaticParams()` — all pages pre-rendered at build time - Docker Swarm deployment with standalone output ### SectionsRenderer Content Resolution **Critical rule for content keys in nexa-pages/*.json:** Components in `sections.tsx` access `pageContent.KEY` (e.g., `HeroSection` does `pageContent.hero`). Components in `FeedbackSection` use `data` directly (e.g., `data.title`). | Component | What it reads | Correct content key | |-----------|--------------|-------------------| | HeroSection | `pageContent.hero` | `"home"` | | TrustSection | `pageContent.trust` | `"home"` | | ServicesSection | `d.services?.groups \|\| d.groups` | `"home"` | | WhyCountrySection | `pageContent.whyCountry` | `"home"` | | ProcessSection | `pageContent.process` | `"home"` | | FeedbackSection | `data.title`, `data.eyebrow` | `"home.feedback"` | | CtaBanner | `pageContent.finalCta \|\| pageContent.cta` | `"home"` | If a section returns null, check the content key depth against what the component reads. ## SectionsRenderer Architecture ### SECTIONS Map All section components are registered in `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx` via the `SECTIONS` record: ```typescript const SECTIONS: Record = { hero: HeroSection, // sections.tsx 'trust-signals': TrustSection, 'why-destination': WhyCountrySection, services: ServicesSection, 'process-timeline': ProcessSection, 'cta-banner': CtaBanner, requirements: RequirementsSection, 'booking-embed': BookingEmbedSection, // sections-extra.tsx contact: ContactDetailsSection, // sections-extra.tsx 'contact-form': ContactDetailsSection, // alias for same component 'contact-details': ContactDetailsSection, 'booking-form': BookingFormSection, // BookingFormSection.tsx } ``` **CRITICAL PITFALL: Unrecognized section IDs → null output.** If a page config references a section ID not in `SECTIONS`, the fallback is `GenericSection`. GenericSection only renders if the resolved data has `items`, `groups`, `pillars`, `members`, `paragraphs`, or `trust.items` arrays. If the data has `title`, `subtitle`, `email`, `whatsapp` (like contact details), GenericSection returns null silently. The page appears empty between header and footer. **Diagnosis:** Check the page's nexa-pages JSON for the `sections[].id` values, then grep SECTIONS in SectionsRenderer.tsx to see if each exists. If a section ID is missing from SECTIONS, the page has no visual feedback — just blank content. **Adding a new section:** 1. Create or import the component in SectionsRenderer.tsx 2. Add the `id` → component mapping to the SECTIONS object 3. The `id` is the camelCase/kebab-case string used in nexa-pages/*.json `sections[].id` ### Locale-Aware Components Section components receive `locale` as a prop. For locale-aware rendering: ```typescript export function BookingFormSection({ data, locale }: any) { const lang = locale || 'es' // Resolve 4-locale data shape: { es: "...", en: "...", nl: "...", de: "..." } const title = typeof d.title === 'object' ? (d.title[lang] || d.title.es) : d.title } ``` The content JSON supports data stored as `{"es": "...", "en": "...", "nl": "...", "de": "..."}` objects. Components MUST resolve at render time using the `locale` prop rather than hardcoding fallback strings. ### Content Data Pollution EN/NL/DE content files can accumulate empty-string keys at every nesting level (artifacts from the content generation pipeline). A `contactPage` object might have 30+ empty `aboutPage: ""` keys alongside `booking` and `contact`. Components that iterate `Object.keys()` on these objects may pick up the pollution. Use explicit property access (`d.title`, `d.items`) rather than iteration. ## Build & Deploy (current) ```bash # On VPS cd /root/nexa-paraguay git pull origin main rm -rf .next docker build --no-cache -t nexa-paraguay:prod . docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web sleep 5 curl -sL https://nexa.paragu-ai.com/es/ | grep -c "Viví el proceso" # verify content renders ``` For clean rebuild (no cache): ```bash docker build --no-cache -t nexa-paraguay:prod . docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web ``` ## CRITICAL PITFALL: JSON Corruption The `patch` tool and `sed` can corrupt large JSON files (content/es.json is 208KB, 900+ lines). A missing comma or broken key breaks the entire site (content: {} empty response — all pages render as header+footer only). **Always validate after every JSON edit:** ```bash python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('content/es.json')); print('VALID')" ``` **Recovery when JSON is corrupt:** 1. The symptom: all pages return `content: {}` in `__NEXT_DATA__`, rendering only header+footer 2. Find the position: `node -e "try{JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('content/es.json','utf8'))}catch(e){console.log(e.message.match(/position (\d+)/)[1])}"` 3. Check around that line: `sed -n '$((POS/80))p' content/es.json` 4. Fix with python json module (not sed/patch): `python3 -c "import json; data=json.load(open('f')); json.dump(data, open('f','w'), ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)"` Safer approach for large edits: use `python3 -c` with `json.load()`/`json.dump()`: ```python import json with open('content/es.json') as f: data = json.load(f) # modify data dict... with open('content/es.json', 'w') as f: json.dump(data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) ``` ## Locale Detection System Two components work together: 1. **Middleware** (`src/middleware.ts`) — redirects bare `/` to `/{locale}/` using `NEXT_LOCALE` cookie (checks `locale` cookie as fallback for old sessions). Read at request time. 2. **getServerSideProps** (`src/pages/[[...slug]].tsx`) — detects locale in priority order: URL prefix → cookie → Accept-Language header. A `hasUrlLocale` flag prevents cookie from overriding explicit URL choice. **Cookie name:** Both must use `NEXT_LOCALE`. The middleware previously read `locale` cookie which never matched the server-set `NEXT_LOCALE` — this caused users to always get English regardless of URL prefix. ## Deployment Notes - `npx next build` locally before pushing to VPS — catch TS errors early - npm install requires --legacy-peer-deps (React 19 + @ai-whisperers mismatch) - Docker build copies .next/standalone — no npm install inside container - Traefik labels in docker-compose.yml at service.deploy.labels level (not service.labels) - Docker service uses agent-net network - Background process on VPS can take 5+ min (build + docker build + service update) - Always `git pull origin main` FIRST if VPS is behind - Check `docker service logs --tail 50 nexa_web` for build errors - Container has no `curl` — use `wget -q -O -` for internal HTTP checks ### Content Not Showing Diagnosis Step 1: Check `__NEXT_DATA__` in page source — look for `content: {` value: - `content: {}` → JSON failed to load (corrupt file or wrong path) - `content: {..."pageId":"home"...}` → content loaded, component may be failing Step 2: Check content key path matches what the component reads: - Page config (nexa-pages/*.json) references `content: "servicesPage.services"` - Component reads `d.groups` from the resolved data - Content JSON must have matching key (e.g., `es.servicesPage.services.groups`) Step 3: Verify JSON validity on VPS: ```bash ssh root@72.61.44.159 "cd /root/nexa-paraguay && python3 -c 'import json; json.load(open(\"content/es.json\")); print(\"VALID\")'" ``` Step 4: Check Docker container has the right content: ```bash docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=nexa_web) ls -la content/es.json docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=nexa_web) node -e "try{JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('content/es.json','utf8'));console.log('VALID')}catch(e){console.log('INVALID:',e.message)}" ``` ## Subagent Timeout Recovery Subagents can timeout at 50 API calls / 600s, leaving dirty git state. Recovery steps: 1. `git diff --stat` — see what was changed 2. `npx next build` — check if partial changes compile 3. If compiles: commit partial work, push, deploy 4. If broken: `git checkout -- path/to/broken/files`, re-apply fixes manually ## Gateway Popup A `GatewayPopup` component at `src/components/GatewayPopup.tsx` renders on first visit (checks `gateway_seen` cookie). Two-step flow: language selection (4 locales) → path selection (relocation/business/investor/info). Re-appears via `?gateway=true` query param. "Reiniciar" link in footer clears the cookie. ## Content Architecture: Homepage vs Dedicated Pages **Critical rule: NO duplicated content between homepage and dedicated pages.** Each piece of content has ONE owner: | Content | Owner Page | Homepage Shows | |---|---|---| | Programs tiers | /programas | Summary headline + CTA link | | Services groups | /servicios | Summary headline + CTA link | | Why Paraguay pillars | /por-que-paraguay | Summary headline + CTA link | | Process steps | /proceso | Summary headline + CTA link | | Testimonials | /casos-de-exito | CTA link only | | Hero, Stats, Trust, Tax Calc, Benelux | / (homepage) | Full content (unique) | ### Summary Mode Pattern Homepage sections use a `ctaText`/`ctaLabel` + `ctaHref` pattern instead of data arrays. Section components detect this: ```typescript // In each section component: if (d.ctaText) { // Summary mode: render headline + subtitle + CTA button return (
...headline...cta...
) } // Full mode: render tiers/groups/steps/etc ``` Implemented for: ProgramsSection, ServicesSection, WhyCountrySection, ProcessSection, TestimonialsSection. ### Content Ownership Map (22 pages) | Path | Content Key | Unique? | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | / | home.* | 6 unique, 5 summary | See table above | | /servicios | servicesPage.* | ✅ | 9 services with full descriptions, benefits, icons | | /programas | programasPage.* | ✅ | 4 tiers, comparison table, FAQ (8 items), testimonials | | /proceso | processPage.* | ✅ | 5 steps with detailParagraph + timeline | | /por-que-paraguay | whyCountryPage.* | ✅ | 4 pillars, trust, faq, process | | /casos-de-exito | caseStudiesPage.* | ✅ | Testimonials + CTAs | | /sobre | aboutPage.* | ✅ | Differentiators, team, trust, gallery, story | | /faq | faqPage.* | ✅ | Full accordion with all questions | | /empresa | landingEmpresa.* | ✅ | Business-focused steps (constitution, RUC, corp account...) | | /inversor | landingInversor.* | ✅ | Investment-focused steps (capital eval, vehicle, due diligence...) | | /lifestyle | lifestyle.* | ✅ | Lifestyle-focused steps (scouting, housing, community...) | | /trust | landingTrust.* | ✅ | Trust-focused steps (credentials, legal audit, compliance...) | | /fundador | founderPage.* | ✅ | | | /comparacion | comparisonPage.* | ✅ | | | /recursos | resourcesPage.* | ✅ | | | /glosario | glossaryPage.* | ✅ | | | /privacidad | privacyPage.* | ✅ | | | /benelux | beneluxPage.* | ✅ | | | /calidad-de-vida | qualityOfLifePage.* | ✅ | | | /prensa | prensa.* | ✅ | | | /asistente | intakeWizardPage.* | ✅ | | | /blog | blog.* | ✅ | 70+ posts | **Live:** https://nexa.paragu-ai.com **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay **Docker service:** nexa_web (1 replica) **Stack:** Next.js 16 App Router, Tailwind v4, Docker Swarm (Traefik), Evolution API ## Completed Improvements (May 8 2026) All of the below plus: - PPR disabled, all routes SSG static HTML - Sections fixed: content keys corrected, TaxCalculatorSection removed - Translation pipeline: weekly Hermes cron (`nexa-translation-pipeline.py`) - Auto-keyword: targetKeyword on all 26 pages + SEO sections - Left here for reference but no longer relevant. - Framework: @ai-whisperers/* ecosystem via npm file: links - Pages Router (Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 5) - Content JSON-driven: content/{locale}.json (4 locales), nexa-pages/*.json (24 page configs), images.json (204 entries) - SSR with 60s in-memory cache via src/lib/loader.ts - SECTION_MAP: 30+ components (sections.tsx + sections-extra.tsx) - SLUG_MAP: 24 page routes mapped to nexa-pages/*.json ## Completed Improvements (May 8 2026) - All 24 page configs fixed with proper id + sections mapping - Blog images fixed, 70+ SEO blog posts across NL/EN - Fabricated stats replaced with honest placeholders - is_demo: false, sitemap.xml + robots.txt + GA4 - Multi-language activation with locale switcher + cookie persistence + Accept-Language detection - Working tax savings calculator (6 countries) - Locale-specific hero images (4 variants) wired via getHeroImage() - OG/favicon/Twitter meta tags in Head - Unicode \\u25be rendering bug fixed - Empty pages filled: servicios (9 service cards), prensa (press releases), asistente (intake wizard) - TeamSection honesty badges on placeholder stock portraits - 4 duplicate landing pages given unique process steps (empresa, inversor, lifestyle, trust) - Gateway popup for first-visit language + path selection - Locale detection: URL prefix always wins over cookie, middleware cookie name fixed ## Content Duplication Audit (May 8 2026) Despite the "NO duplicated content" rule, several sections render the same data across multiple pages. These are structural duplicates — the same content path is referenced in different page configs, producing identical visible output. ### Confirmed Duplicates | Content | Pages | Same Items? | Difference | |---|---|---|---| | **Services** (9 items, 3 groups) | `/` (homepage) + `/en/servicios` | YES — identical names, descriptions, grouping | Homepage has `home.services` (no benefit checklists), servicios has `servicesPage.services` (with ✓ lists). But same 9 titles creates duplicate feel | | **Process steps** (5 steps) | `/` (homepage) + `/en/proceso` | YES — identical titles, descriptions | Process page adds "Week 1" / "Weeks 2–5" timeline labels. Steps are otherwise word-for-word identical | | **Pricing tiers** (4 tiers) | `/` (homepage) + `/en/programas` | YES — same USD prices, same features | 4th tier name: "Land Purchase" (home) vs "Paraguay Land Program" (programas). Otherwise identical | | **Our Story** (3 paragraphs) | `/en/sobre` + `/en/fundador` | YES — exact same text | Team section differs (5 people vs 3). Story is identical | ### Detection Method 1. **List all page configs**: `ls nexa-pages/*.json` — extract `sections[].content` paths 2. **Check same content path across pages**: `content_path_usage` dict keyed by content path, values = list of (page, section_id) 3. **Check structurally identical data under different paths**: compare json-serialized `d.get(path).groups[].items[].title` — don't stop at first-level keys, drill into the actual content arrays 4. **Scrape live pages**: compare rendered HTML for identical section patterns (same section heading + same item titles) ### Fix Strategy For homepage sections that duplicate dedicated pages: replace the full data array with a **summary card + CTA link**: ```typescript // Summary mode — not another full grid if (d.ctaText) { return (

{d.title}

{d.subtitle}

{d.ctaText}
) } ``` This is already partially implemented for `ProgramsSection`, `ServicesSection`, `WhyCountrySection`, `ProcessSection`, `TestimonialsSection` — they have `ctaText`/`ctaHref` support. The fix is to update the homepage content JSON to USE `ctaText` instead of full arrays. ### Priority Order 1. **homepage services** → `ctaText` summary (most visible duplicate) 2. **homepage process** → `ctaText` summary (process page already has better content) 3. **about/founder story** → rewrite one as company-scale vs founder-personal 4. **homepage programs** → `ctaText` summary (programs page has FAQ + testimonials) ## Living Upgrade Plan The full 75-idea upgrade plan for Nexa Paraguay lives at: **https://github.com/Ai-Whisperers/infrastructure/blob/main/nexa-paraguay-upgrade-plan.md** 10 categories: Architecture, Content/Localization, SEO, Integrations, Hermes, Code Quality, Monetization, Security, Documentation, Strategic. {"action": "patch", "file_path": "SKILL.md", "name": "nexa-paraguay-deployment", "new_string": "### Locale-Aware Components

Section components receive `locale` as a prop. For locale-aware rendering:

```typescript
export function BookingFormSection({ data, locale }: any) {
  const lang = locale || 'es'
  // Resolve 4-locale data shape: { es: "...", en: "...", nl: "...", de: "..." }
  const title = typeof d.title === 'object' ? (d.title[lang] || d.title.es) : d.title
}
```

The content JSON supports data stored as `{"es": "...", "en": "...", "nl": "...", "de": "..."}` objects. Components MUST resolve at render time using the `locale` prop rather than hardcoding fallback strings.

**Pattern: Locale-filtered hours display** — When an object like `{ "Mon-Fri": "...", "Sat": "...", "Sun": "...", "Lun-Vie": "...", ... }` has keys per locale, filter with a locale-key map:

```typescript
const localeHoursKeys = {
  es: ['Lun-Vie', 'Sáb', 'Dom'],
  en: ['Mon-Fri', 'Sat', 'Sun'],
  nl: ['Ma-Vr', 'Za', 'Zo'],
  de: ['Mo-Fr', 'Sa', 'So'],
}
const keys = localeHoursKeys[locale || 'es']
const hours = keys.map(k => d.hours[k]).filter(Boolean).join(' · ')
```

**Content data shape for BookingFormSection** — The `contactPage.booking` content path should provide:

| Field | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|

| `step1Title`..`step3Title` | string | Step headings per locale |
| `nameLabel`, `emailLabel`, `phoneLabel` | string | Input labels |
| `step1Subtitle`..`step3Subtitle` | string | Step descriptions |
| `confirmTitle`, `confirmText` | string | Post-submit confirmation |", "old_string": "### Locale-Aware Components
